## What’s Next in Quantum is quantum-centricsupercomputing
IBM is bringing useful quantum computing to the world. IBM offers the world's most powerful quantum computers via the cloud and powered by Qiskit, the quantum software stack built for performance. Our unrivaled research, fabrication facilities, and velocity of innovation have allowed us to advance along our roadmap toward quantum advantage in 2026 and fault tolerant quantum computing in 2029. Joining us on this journey are 250+ Fortune 500 companies, academic institutions, national labs, and startups comprising our IBM Quantum Network, all striving toward the common goal of realizing quantum advantage in their domains.

Qiskit is the world’s most popular and performant software stack for quantum computing and algorithms research. Build, optimize, and execute quantum workloads at scale.

- [01About](#about)
- [02Parts of Qiskit](#tools-and-services)
- [03Applied tutorials](#tutorials)
- [04Qiskit ecosystem](#ecosystem)
- [05Community resources](#get-involved)
- [06Qiskit advocates](#advocates)
- [07Latest updates](#latest-updates)

## Open-source quantum software for large-scale execution

- 13MQiskit downloads
- 69%Developers prefer Qiskit as primary SDK, according to the [Unitary Foundation](https://unitaryfoundation.github.io/survey-2025/#full-stack-development-platforms-compilers-and-simulators-used-currently-or-in-the-future)
- 83xFaster transpilation time, according to [Benchpress toolkit](https://github.com/Qiskit/benchpress)
- 7K+Dependent projects
Qiskit is the software of choice for the era of advantage because it offers the most comprehensive suite of open-source tools for advancing research across the stack. Experiment at scale with the world’s fastest transpiler, which prepares workloads 83x faster than the next leading SDK.

### Explore what’s possible with Qiskit

- #### Quantum information science
Power tools for designing quantum experiments and new capabilities.[Learn more](#tools-science)
- #### Heterogeneous orchestration
Qiskit plugins support orchestration of quantum and classical resources.[Learn more](#tools-hpc)
- #### Algorithm development
Tools map problems to circuits and prepare them for execution.[Learn more](#tools-algorithm)

## Tools for quantum information science
Qiskit offers open-source power tools and a streamlined workflow to help researchers compose utility-scale experiments.

### Quantum information science toolkit

Qiskit has tools for working with quantum computers at the level of extended quantum circuits, operators, and primitives. It is 83x faster at transpiling and producing circuits with 29% fewer two-qubit gates than the next leading competitor.

- Build quantum circuits from quantum gates
- Compile circuits for execution
- Use primitives to build and execute experiments
- Visualize circuits and analyze results

### Benchmark quantum software performance

Use the Benchpress open-source toolkit to evaluate software performance across various quantum SDKs.

[View repo](https://github.com/Qiskit/benchpress)

## Tools for heterogeneous orchestration
Qiskit plugins connect quantum compute resources to existing HPC workload managers for orchestrated quantum-classical executions.

### Qiskit quantum hardware plugins

Qiskit is open-source and backend-agnostic, supporting quantum execution across a broad range of popular hardware providers.

[View all](/quantum/ecosystem?tag=Compute+provider)

### Quantum resource management

The quantum resource management interface (QRMI) standardizes and simplifies control of quantum resources.

[View repo](https://github.com/qiskit-community/spank-plugins/tree/main/plugins/spank_qrmi)

### Slurm plugin

This plugin allows you to manage quantum resources using the Slurm workload manager.

[View repo](https://github.com/qiskit-community/spank-plugins)

### Qiskit Serverless

A simple interface to run workloads across quantum-classical resources, supporting multi-cloud and supercomputing use cases.

[View repo](https://github.com/Qiskit/qiskit-serverless)

## Tools for algorithm development
Qiskit offers modular tools for designing and customizing algorithms with as little overhead as possible. Save time by using Qiskit addons and function templates to accelerate your work.

### Combine addons to build algorithms

Qiskit addons are a collection of algorithmic building blocks for enabling algorithm discovery at utility scale. Combine these modular capabilities as part of your algorithm design workflow.

### Save time with pre-built code templates

Qiskit Function templates are realistic code examples that implement the latest research workflows with Qiskit and Qiskit addons.

Explore quantum approaches to combinatorial problems that challenge existing classical methods.

Use quantum and HPC to simulate complex systems in nature across physics and chemistry domains.

}

content.text:
Based on my research, I can now compile a comprehensive summary of quantum computing's state as of March 2026.

---

## Quantum Computing State of the Union — March 2026

### 1. Hardware Breakthroughs

| Company | Development | Status |
|---------|-------------|--------|
| **Rigetti** | New adiabatic CZ entangling gate achieving **99.9% two-qubit gate fidelity** at 28ns gate speed | Demonstrated March 2026 |
| **Rigetti** | **Cepheus-1-108Q** (108-qubit system) | General availability Q1 2026 |
| **IonQ** | 100-qubit system for KISTI (South Korea) | Delivery 2025 |
| **IBM** | Quantum-centric supercomputing deployment | Active with RIKEN/Fugaku |
| **Microsoft** | **Majorana 1 chip** using topoconductor material | Announced; claims path to millions of qubits |

**Source: IBM Research homepage, Rigetti March 4 2026 press release, Azure Quantum page**

### 2. Major Players & Strategic Moves

**IonQ** (Trapped Ion):
- Acquired SkyWater Technology for **$1.8B** (Jan 26, 2026) — creating only vertically-integrated full-stack quantum platform
- Acquired Oxford Ionics (~$1B), Skyloom (quantum networking), and Seed Innovations (AI R&D)
- Achieved **ISO 14001 certification** (March 3, 2026)
- Selected for **U.S. Missile Defense Agency SHIELD program**
- Delivered operational Quantum Key Distribution network in Europe (Feb 26, 2026)

**Rigetti** (Superconducting):
- $8.4M order from India's C-DAC for 108-qubit system
- Revised roadmap: Cepheus-1-108Q launching end of Q1 2026
- Developed proprietary high-fidelity gates targeting **quantum error correction (QEC)**

**IBM** (Superconducting):
- Target: **Quantum advantage in 2026**, fault-tolerant computing in **2029**
- Qiskit ecosystem: 13M+ downloads, 83x faster transpilation than competitors
- 250+ Fortune 500 companies in IBM Quantum Network
- **RIKEN/Fugaku** collaboration demonstrating quantum-centric supercomputing at scale

**Microsoft** (Topological):
- Claims **Majorana 1 chip** breakthrough with topoconductor material
- Aims for path to **millions of physical qubits** on single chip

**Source: IonQ newsroom, Rigetti press releases, IBM Research blog**

### 3. Quantum Advantage Milestones

IBM has established a public **"Quantum Advantage Tracker"** (Feb 23, 2026, per IBM blog) actively tracking the race to quantum advantage across industry and research.

Multiple companies (IBM, IonQ) targeting **2026** as the year for first demonstrated quantum advantage in practical applications. Microsoft's Majorana 1 announcement positions them on a longer path to **large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computing**.

**Source: IBM Quantum Advantage Tracker blog**

### 4. Practical Applications & Use Cases

| Domain | Current Activity |
|--------|----------------|
| **Cybersecurity/Networking** | IonQ deployed operational QKD networks in Europe and Slovakia's first national quantum communication network |
| **Defense/Government** | IonQ selected for Missile Defense Agency; U.S.-Italy cooperation on risk analysis/civil protection |
| **HPC Integration** | IBM/RIKEN demonstrating quantum-centric supercomputing at Fugaku scale |
| **Chemistry/Materials** | IBM simulating half-Möbius molecule properties |
| **Financial Services** | Exact simulation of quantum-enhanced signature kernels (Rigetti + Amazon Braket) |

**Source: IonQ press releases Dec 2025-Mar 2026, IBM Research publications**

### 5. Key Challenges & Limitations

1. **Qubit Count vs. Quality Trade-off**: Rigetti's 108-qubit system vs. IonQ's ~100+ ion trap approach shows different architectures competing
2. **Gate Fidelity**: 99.9% two-qubit fidelity is the new bar (Rigetti), but still requires ~10,000+ for fault-tolerant computing
3. **Roadmap Slippage**: Rigetti revised Cepheus-1-108Q timeline from earlier estimates
4. **Integration**: Heavy focus on hybrid quantum-classical systems (IBM's quantum-centric supercomputing)
5. **Standardization**: ISO 14001 certification for IonQ suggests industry is moving toward enterprise-ready standards

---

## Near-Term Outlook (2026-2027)

**What to Watch:**
- **Q1/Q2 2026**: Rigetti Cepheus-1-108Q GA; continued IBM "advantage" claims
- **Consolidation**: IonQ's acquisition spree suggests industry consolidation
- **Error Correction**: Multiple players targeting QEC-ready systems with 99.9%+ fidelity gates
- **International Expansion**: South Korea, India, Italy all deploying quantum infrastructure

**Bottom Line**: As of March 2026, quantum computing is in the **"utility-scale" era** — beyond pure research, with multiple vendors offering 100+ qubit systems and seeking practical advantages in optimization, simulation, and security applications. The path to **fault-tolerant quantum computing** (2029 per IBM) remains the north star.
